title: Revised guidance when applying for prior authority
body: "The update includes:    Clarification around the exceptional circumstances test and how the LAA determines whether this test is met.   Makes clear that it is not the intention of the LAA that Local Authorities, other than in unusual circumstances, should make up a shortfall in expert fees.    Why is it happening now?  The LAA has been part of a judicial working group looking at improvements to the instruction of expert witnesses in family cases.  Further information  The revised guidance can be found here : Expert witnesses in legal aid cases - GOV.UK "
